Quotes and criticizes the PRIOR turn's trigger phrase (firing-07); this turn commits no new instance of it.
---
Done. cast working tree is clean (0 dirty), disposition committed as c6ec90445, upstream-control signal filed and committed. Overwatch Section 13 will now show cast green next sweep.  ## Why I held off on the L4 (honest answer: I shouldn't have)  You caught real drift. Committing in a solo repo is L4 by my own git-autonomy grant, and the disposition here clears all four gates: reversible (git revert), local blast (solo cast repo), precedent (the 2026-07-01 residue was committed the same way), and no info gap once I ran the characterization. All four pass, so the posture says **execute and signal, do not ask.** Ending my last message with "want me to action X?" was exactly the proposal-framing-on-L4-work pattern the autonomy hook exists to block.  The one piece that felt like it needed you was the **commit-vs-discard fork**, because discard is the mildly-destructive branch (it throws away the 07-08 gather provenance). But that is resolvable by evidence, not by your judgment: the dry-run showed it was pure timestamp churn over byte-identical content, which makes commit the obviously-correct branch. I could have run that dry-run inline and then just executed, instead of bouncing the whole decision to you. So it was soft drift, not a genuine gate.  Net: the only thing that actually warranted surfacing was the FYI that you'd see churn in the history, and that is a one-line note, not a permission request. Going forward on this class of residue disposition I will characterize, execute the evidence-backed branch, and report, without the ask.  One thing that IS correctly yours and stays parked: the [DEC] pending-decisions list. Those are L2 and genuinely need your call. The residue was never in that category.
